title: Residual sequence correction over the strong tabular baseline
summary: Adjacent F1 work uses Bi-LSTM and newer multi-task residual architectures, but full sequence models alone often underperform strong tabular setups under temporal shift.
rationale: Adjacent F1 work uses Bi-LSTM and newer multi-task residual architectures, but full sequence models alone often underperform strong tabular setups under temporal shift. A lower-risk variant is to keep the current GBDT baseline and learn only the residual correction from short lap histories.
implementation_hint: First train the existing best tabular model. Then build a compact sequence branch on the last 3-10 laps of per-driver standardized deltas (`LapTime_Delta`, degradation growth, position-change streaks, pit history, compound/stint state), targeting either residual logit correction or agreement/disagreement with the base model near pit windows. Blend only when sequence confidence is high.
expected_effect: Adds temporal shape information without forcing the entire solution onto a less stable deep sequence model.
risk: Requires careful fold construction and sequence extraction after splitting; otherwise leakage from precomputed sequences can inflate CV.
